mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of the broader macro regime.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative FEDFUNDS trend periods show no significant excess return versus unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers rapid risk-off repricing in equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of UNRATE spikes fail to produce negative median 5-day forward returns, or more than 55% of triggered instances show positive returns, across the walk-forward evaluation sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer demand strength that is not captured by either series alone; joint positive momentum in both series predicts sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days) as earnings expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint acceleration episodes fail to outperform single-series signals in out-of-sample Rank IC by a statistically meaningful margin, or 60-day forward returns following joint triggers are not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A persistent uptrend in INDPRO over the trailing 6 months signals expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, which supports forward earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 6-month trend and forward 20-day SPX return remains below 0.03 across any 2-year rolling window, or trend-positive periods do not show statistically higher mean forward returns than trend-negative periods.

mechanism_narrative: When month-over-month PAYEMS net job gains fall below 100,000, this signals an inflection toward labor market weakness that markets have not yet priced, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as growth expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100,000 persons
failure_condition: Triggered low-payroll-growth episodes show positive median forward 5-day returns, or fewer than 55% of triggered instances show negative returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation is also decelerating, the combination signals a disinflationary easing cycle in which real rates decline faster than nominal rates, compressing equity risk premia and producing strong positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). Neither series alone captures this real-rate effect.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines over trailing 3 months AND CPIAUCSL YoY inflation decelerates over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL disinflation-easing episodes fail to outperform baseline unconditional 60-day forward returns at p > 0.05, or the interaction signal's Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series signal's Rank IC by a material margin.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 above trend reflects durable real economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 QoQ growth and forward 60-day SPX return falls below 0.03 across any rolling 3-year window, or above-trend GDP growth periods do not show significantly higher forward returns than below-trend periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle where FEDFUNDS has risen sharply over the trailing 12 months, a subsequent pause or reversal (FEDFUNDS flattening or declining for 2 consecutive months) signals the end of tightening and is interpreted by markets as a dovish pivot, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect is specific to tightening-cycle-end regimes and does not hold during easing or stable-rate regimes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rose over trailing 12 months AND then flattens or declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Pivot-trigger episodes identified within tightening cycles fail to show significantly higher forward 20-day returns than non-triggered tightening-cycle periods (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one distinct tightening cycle in the historical sample.
